title: JudgeScores
description: Output schema for the blind LLM judge. The judge sees two outputs labelled A and B (random assignment) and scores each against a 5-criterion rubric. The mapping field de-anonymises A/B back to the provider names for human review.

verdict.description: Overall judgment after weighing all five criteria together. 'tie' is allowed and expected when scores are within 1 point across most criteria.

verdict_reasoning.description: 2-4 sentences explaining the overall verdict. Reference specific criteria and quote evidence.

close_calls.description: List of criterion names where the two outputs were within 1 point. These are surfaced for human spot-checking.

mapping.description: De-anonymisation: which provider's output was shown as A and which as B.

source_type_audit.description: URL-pattern spot-check of citations.source_type. The judge does not trust the provider's self-tag; it independently classifies a sample of citations and reports mismatches. Used to refine the citation_quality score.

score.description: 1 = poor, 2 = below average, 3 = adequate, 4 = good, 5 = excellent.

reasoning.description: 2-3 sentences with specific evidence (quote a field path or citation index).

citations_sampled.description: How many citations the judge independently classified.

mismatches.description: Citations where the judge's URL-pattern classification disagreed with the provider's self-tag. An over-claim of 'primary' on an aggregator URL is the dominant failure mode to catch.

url_pattern_evidence.description: Short note on which URL pattern led the judge to disagree (e.g., 'wikipedia.org → aggregator, not primary'; 'reuters.com → secondary, not primary').
